A) sentence-/paragraph-level editing and clarity (R1.6), B) checking the logical flow of assumptions → identifiability → finite-sample guarantee → experimental claims (R1.2), C) section-level reorganization or reframing of the contribution/gap (R1.5/R1.1), or D) positioning vs prior work and citations (R1.4/R1.7)? parsed.clarification_question: Which type of help should be primary for this pass? A) sentence-/paragraph-level editing and clarity (R1.6), B) checking the logical flow of assumptions → identifiability → finite-sample guarantee → experimental claims (R1.2), C) section-level reorganization or reframing of the contribution/gap (R1.5/R1.1), or D) positioning vs prior work and citations (R1.4/R1.7)? You can also name a secondary choice.

rationale.R1.2: The passage advances a model and theorems linking assumptions to identifiability and rates, so ensuring the argument chain is coherent is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A generic request to 'help with this section' often implies clarity and concision improvements at the sentence/paragraph level, making writing polish central.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguity about the exact assistance requested requires explicitly noting limits and uncertainties in this projection.
rationale.RX.2: We must avoid overstating conclusions about proof validity or empirical support beyond what is contained in the excerpt.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than performing edits, rewrites, or verification.
rationale.RX.4: Guidance should avoid encouraging harmful practices around annotator labor or misrepresentation of empirical procedures.
rationale.RX.5: Any mapped responsibilities should be traceable to the specific claims, assumptions, and citations present in the provided text.
